prompt:
Our field-inspection intake service handles upload posts from the tablets itself — stdlib only, no framework — and since we went live two kinds of tickets keep landing on me and I'm done playing whack-a-mole.

One: attachments sometimes arrive truncated. A QA engineer uploads her saved email digest (a perfectly normal .eml she exported from the mail client) and what lands in storage is a fraction of the file — the first line or two, then nothing. Another customer's saved web-page archive does the same thing, AND on that submission the email address field they typed into the form just... isn't there on our side; the review dashboard shows the attachment (truncated) and a blank where the email should be. Support keeps writing these off as 'flaky tablet wifi', but the same file fails the same way every single time, on every retry, from every device — that is not packet loss. Meanwhile thousands of photo uploads a week are flawless, so I can't just say 'uploads are broken' either.

Two: anyone with an accent anywhere in their submission gets mangled. An inspector named Renée shows up as 'RenÃ©e' on the dashboard, 'Zürich' becomes 'ZÃ¼rich', and a customer's French PDF gets stored under a filename full of Ã© goop. The tablets are modern browsers-in-a-webview posting bog-standard forms; nobody is sending anything exotic.

I pulled raw request bodies for a handful of the bad submissions and turned them into test_formdata.py — `python3 test_formdata.py` replays them and fails exactly the way production does (plus it re-checks the everyday cases that DO work today, which must keep working). Those captures are ground truth: fix the intake code so the replay passes, and do not touch the test file.
